description: Add claw-forge export command to export session and task data from state.db in CSV, SQL, or JSON format. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-export-command-design.md

userStories.title: Create exporter module with CSV flat export
userStories.description:
As a user, I want to run claw-forge export and get a CSV file with one row per task, session fields denormalized into each row.

Create claw_forge/exporter.py with export_csv_flat(db_path, session_id, out) that reads state.db via sqlite3 (not SQLAlchemy), joins sessions+tasks, writes CSV with columns: session_id, project_path, session_status, session_created_at, task_id, plugin_name, description, category, status, priority, depends_on, steps, error_message, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, created_at, started_at, completed_at. depends_on and steps are semicolon-separated strings.
acceptanceCriteria: claw_forge/exporter.py exists with export_csv_flat function
Function reads DB via sqlite3, not SQLAlchemy
Output CSV has correct columns with session fields denormalized
depends_on and steps serialized as semicolon-separated strings
Null values are empty strings
Returns the output Path
Unit tests pass (uv run pytest tests/ -q)
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/)

userStories.title: Add CSV split, SQL, and JSON export functions
userStories.description:
As a user, I want multiple export formats.

Add to claw_forge/exporter.py:
- export_csv_split(db_path, session_id, out_dir): one CSV per table (sessions.csv, tasks.csv, events.csv) in a directory
- export_sql(db_path, session_id, out): SQLite dump with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + INSERT statements, filtered by session_id when provided
- export_json(db_path, session_id, out): JSON matching API shape {exported_at, claw_forge_version, scope, sessions: [{..., tasks: [...]}]}
acceptanceCriteria: export_csv_split creates directory with sessions.csv, tasks.csv, events.csv
export_sql produces valid SQL importable via sqlite3 newdb.db < export.sql
export_json produces JSON matching the spec shape with nested sessions→tasks
All functions accept session_id=None for full dump
All functions return the output Path
Unit tests cover each format
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/)

userStories.title: Add CLI command claw-forge export
userStories.description:
As a user, I want to run claw-forge export from the terminal.

Add Typer command to cli.py:
- --format/-f csv|sql|json (default csv)
- --scope session|all (default session)
- --session/-s UUID (default: latest via _resolve_latest_session)
- --csv-mode flat|split (default flat)
- --out/-o PATH (default: auto-generated)
- --project/-p (default .)

Auto-generated filenames: claw-forge-export-{session_id[:8]}-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.{ext}
For --scope all: claw-forge-export-all-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.{ext}

Print confirmation with format, row counts, and output path.
acceptanceCriteria: claw-forge export --help shows all flags
Default: exports current session as flat CSV
--format sql produces SQL dump
--format json produces JSON export
--scope all exports all sessions
--csv-mode split creates directory with per-table CSVs
Auto-generated filenames follow the spec pattern
Error: DB not found shows clear message
Error: session not found lists available sessions
Full test suite passes (uv run pytest tests/ -q)
Coverage stays >= 90%
Lint passes (uv run ruff check claw_forge/ tests/)
userStories.notes: Add to claw_forge/cli.py, add CLI tests to tests/test_cli_commands.py or tests/test_exporter.py
